topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed, and a jolt of pure, unadulterated glee shot through me. A notification. A friend request. My breath hitched, and I couldn't stop the wide grin stretching across my face. It was from Mark. Mark, the architect of my early-morning misery, the king of tripping me in the schoolyard. My fingers flew across the screen, a frantic, happy tapping as I accepted. A little surge of adrenaline made my chest feel light, like I could float. I immediately started composing a message, my thumbs dancing with excitement. "Hey, Mark! Long time no see! How are things?" I typed, leaning back in my chair, practically vibrating with anticipation. I could already picture the reunion, the sheer, glorious chaos of reconnecting with that familiar, if slightly irritating, figure.

[story 2]

The screen lit up, and I nearly dropped my mug. A friend request. It was him. Liam. The one whose shadow I’d tried desperately to outrun for years. A sudden warmth flooded my system, chasing away any lingering doubt. I shot up from the sofa, my movements quick and springy. I bounced on the balls of my feet, a bubbling energy making my whole body feel electric. *He wants to talk?* I thought, a laugh bubbling up in my throat. I clicked accept before I could second-guess myself, a giddy sense of possibility swirling inside me. I started pacing the room, my eyes sparkling as I formulated the perfect, over-the-top greeting. This was going to be amazing.

[story 3]

When the notification appeared, I actually let out a small, involuntary squeal. It was a sound so bright it startled the cat. It was him. That notorious kid from elementary school who specialized in making everyone else feel small. A huge smile broke across my face, pulling my cheeks taut. I practically leaped to the window, needing to feel the air rush past me, needing to express this sudden, overwhelming sense of delight. I navigated to his profile, my heart thumping a rapid, joyful rhythm against my ribs. I typed a message, my fingers moving with an almost reckless abandon. "Mark! Wow! This is incredible!" I finished, leaning my head against the glass, completely alight with buoyant happiness.
